title: Kultaranta Talks 2026 begin on 11 June
content:
Kultaranta Talks hosted and organized by the President of the Republic of Finland in cooperation with Finnish Institute of International Affairs will begin next Thursday, 11 June. The Institute’s Director, Hiski Haukkala, will participate on Friday, 12 June in a panel discussion focusing on Russia and its future.

Below is press release 40/2026 from the Office of the President of the Republic of Finland.

On 11 and 12 June, President of the Republic of Finland Alexander Stubb will host the 2026 Kultaranta Talks at Kultaranta in Naantali. The theme of this year’s discussions on foreign and security policy is A World in Transition – Global, Regional and Local Perspectives.

The two-day event will open with an international day focusing on geopolitical and local developments, as well as their interplay across and within different regions, including Africa, the Middle East, Asia, Europe and the United States. Journalists Zanny Minton Beddoes and Gideon Rachman will moderate the discussions.

The second day of the event is dedicated to examining international interactions from a Finnish perspective. Journalist Olli Seuri will serve as the moderator.

title: Programme Director Harri Mikkola appointed Acting Deputy Director of FIIA
content: Programme Director Harri Mikkola has been appointed Acting Deputy Director of FIIA from 1 June to 31 October 2026.
snippet: Programme Director Harri Mikkola has been appointed Acting Deputy Director of FIIA from 1 June to 31 October 2026.
creator: Anna Kananen
sourceTitle: The Finnish Institute of International Affairs (FIIA)

title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: Geo-economic resilience
content:
New publication in the NB8 policy brief series by Thorsteinn Kristinsson (IIA) and Ludvig Broomé (UI)

Geo-economic competition and the coercive use of economic statecraft are supplanting the rules-based system of liberal economic exchange. Small economies like the NB8 states are vulnerable to this development, including the potential weaponisation of external dependencies against them. This policy brief proposes measures for NB8 states to prepare for such scenarios and suggests collective policies to enhance geo-economic resilience in the region.

This policy brief series aims to deepen understanding of the evolving security, political, and societal dynamics that shape the Nordic-Baltic region amid renewed great-power competition.
snippet: New publication in the NB8 policy brief series by Thorsteinn Kristinsson (IIA) and Ludvig Broomé (UI)
creator: Meeri Väätäinen
sourceTitle: The Finnish Institute of International Affairs (FIIA)

title: FIIA takes part in European Studies Day 11 May
content:
The Centre for European Studies at the University of Helsinki hosts a European Studies Day on 11 May 2026. The event brings together researchers of Europe and the EU from various academic disciplines to present recent research in the field.

The Finnish Institute of International Affairs will organise two panels as part of the event. The first panel will examine how different policy sectors function as tools of geopolitics. The second panel will discuss how successful the EU is in responding to the era of power politics.

The event will take place at the Language Centre of the University of Helsinki (Fabianinkatu 26).
snippet: The Centre for European Studies at the University of Helsinki hosts a European Studies Day on 11 May 2026. The event brings together researchers of Europe and the EU from various academic disciplines
creator: Meeri Väätäinen
sourceTitle: The Finnish Institute of International Affairs (FIIA)

title: Deputy Director Samu Paukkunen will move to a new role starting 1 June
content:
Samu Paukkunen, Deputy Director of the Finnish Institute of International Affairs, has resigned from his position and will move to the energy company Fortum as of 1 June 2026. Paukkunen will take up the role of Director, geopolitics and resilience at Fortum.

The vacant position will be opened for public application at a later date.

The Finnish Institute of International Affairs warmly congratulates Samu Paukkunen on his new role!

More information:
Deputy Director Samu Paukkunen
samu.paukkunen@fiia.fi
09 432 7702

title: Apply for an internship at the Finnish Institute of International Affairs
content:
The Finnish Institute of International Affairs is seeking three university interns for research assistant positions for summer 2026.

  • The first research assistant will work in the Institute’s European Union and Strategic Competition research programme, from 1 June to 31 August 2026
  • The second research assistant will work in the Finland and Northern European Security research programme, from 1 June to 31 August 2026
  • The third research assistant will work in the Global Security and Governance research programme, from 1 June to 31 August 2026

    • The application period ends on 11 May 2026 at 16:15. Please visit the job advertisement on the valtiolle.fi website.

    You can also read more about internships and the recruitment of interns on the page Internships at the Finnish Institute of International Affairs.
snippet: The Finnish Institute of International Affairs is seeking three university interns for research assistant positions for summer 2026.
creator: Meeri Väätäinen
sourceTitle: The Finnish Institute of International Affairs (FIIA)

title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: The circles of security
content:
Russia’s war against Ukraine has profoundly worsened Europe’s security environment, but it has also spurred deeper regional cooperation. The challenge now is to ensure that this growing number of initiatives remains purposeful, adds concrete value, and strengthens both the EU and NATO.

This policy brief series aims to deepen understanding of the evolving security, political, and societal dynamics that shape the Nordic-Baltic region amid renewed great-power competition. 

The series is jointly published by the Finnish Institute of International Affairs (FIIA), the Danish Institute for International Studies (DIIS), the Icelandic Institute of International Affairs (IIA), the International Centre for Defence and Security (ICDS), the Institute of International Relations and Political Science at Vilnius University (TSPMI), the Latvian Institute of International Affairs (LIIA), the Norwegian Institute of International Affairs (NUPI), and the Swedish Institute of International Affairs (UI).

title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: How to implement a full maritime services ban on Russian oil
content:
The EU is considering a full maritime services ban on Russian oil. While the final proposal is still under negotiation, the measure would replace the current oil price-cap regime by prohibiting European and allied companies from providing maritime services for Russian oil exports, regardless of the sale price. The ban would cover transportation, insurance, financing, and other related services. This brief outlines how such a full services ban could be designed and implemented effectively, and how to address potential negative side effects and potential Russian countermeasures.

This policy brief series aims to deepen understanding of the evolving security, political, and societal dynamics that shape the Nordic-Baltic region amid renewed great-power competition.

title: FIIA welcomes invitations to public events
content:
To mark the Finnish Institute of International Affairs’ 65th anniversary, FIIA researchers will be taking part in public events across Finland to discuss the most important current issues in foreign and security policy.

We welcome invitations to a wide range of events and venues where a discussion on foreign policy would be a good fit for the programme.

FIIA offers top-level expertise across many fields of international politics, including Finnish foreign and security policy, Russia, the EU, transatlantic relations, European defence, climate policy and NATO. We are also happy to consider other topic suggestions.

If you know of an event or venue where a discussion on foreign policy would be of interest, please let us know by emailing events@fiia.fi.

title: Study: Russia’s influence activities exploited special characteristics of bilateral relationship with Finland
content:
A study carried out by the Finnish Institute of International Affairs together with the University of Helsinki and the University of Tampere examined Russia’s full-spectrum influence activities directed at Finland since the 2000s and their effects on Finnish society and decision-making.

The government-funded research project is based on the Government Programme and a description of information needs published by the Government.

According to the study, Russia’s influence activities have capitalised on specific characteristics of the bilateral relationship between Russia and Finland, and this has partly been successful. Certain features of Finnish political culture – such as a strong preference for consensus and caution in the public debate around Russia – have made it more difficult to identify indirect forms of influence, particularly in the context of energy and investment-related issues. By contrast, more coercive forms of Russian influence, such as the instrumentalisation of migration, have generally proved less effective. Finland’s ability to combat these forms of influence has been supported by its comprehensive security model and strong culture of security policy consensus.
snippet: A study carried out by the Finnish Institute of International Affairs together with the University of Helsinki and the University of Tampere examined Russia’s full-spectrum influence activities
creator: Meeri Väätäinen
sourceTitle: The Finnish Institute of International Affairs (FIIA)
